creator: tkato@prontomail.ne.jp
text:
> I haven't tried the program, but I've read the documentation for it, and I
> gather that it is non-interactive.  I feel that things which can't be
> played do not belong in the "games" category.  This program seems to be
> similar to xantfarm or xmascot, and less similar to gbackbround, qrash,
> xaniroc, xballoon, xsnow, xstarroll, or xtacy--all of which are in "x11"
> and none of which are in "games".  Perhaps there are enough of these to
> justify an "amusements" (or similar) category.  I'd be in favor of that.

According to previous discussions at ports list, It seems X programs
which provide no real productivity should be categorized as 'games'.
Intaractivity is not the point.

For example, xbaby, xblood, xfireworks, xoj, xroach, and xsheep belong
in the 'games' category. Particulaly, xsheep has been moved from 'x11'
to 'games'. Since xpenguins resembles closely to xsheep and xroach,
'games' would be suitable for this program. I suppose xantfarm, at
least, belongs in inappropriate category....

BTW, I couldn't succeed to run xpenguins without applying official
patch from its master site. Therefore, I tried to rebuild port skelton
to use that patch with fixing some more part. Please review it.

# This is a shell archive.  Save it in a file, remove anything before
# this line, and then unpack it by entering "sh file".  Note, it may
# create directories; files and directories will be owned by you and
# have default permissions.
#
# This archive contains:
#
#	xpenguins
#	xpenguins/Makefile
#	xpenguins/distinfo
#	xpenguins/files
#	xpenguins/files/patch-aa
#	xpenguins/pkg-comment
#	xpenguins/pkg-descr
#	xpenguins/pkg-plist
#
echo c - xpenguins
mkdir -p xpenguins > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - xpenguins/Makefile
sed 's/^X//' >xpenguins/Makefile << 'END-of-xpenguins/Makefile'
X# New ports collection makefile for:	xpenguins
X# Date created:		24 January 2001
X# Whom:			lyngbol@candid.dk
X#
X# $FreeBSD$
X#
X
XPORTNAME=	xpenguins
XPORTVERSION=	1.2
XCATEGORIES=	games
XMASTER_SITES=	http://xpenguins.seul.org/
X
XPATCH_SITES=	${MASTER_SITES}
XPATCHFILES=	${PORTNAME}-${PORTVERSION}-xpm_valuemask_fix.patch
XPATCH_DIST_STRIP=	-p1
X
XMAINTAINER=	lyngbol@candid.dk
X
XUSE_X_PREFIX=	yes
XUSE_XPM=	yes
X
XMAN1=		xpenguins.1
X
Xdo-install:
X	${INSTALL_PROGRAM} ${WRKSRC}/xpenguins ${PREFIX}/bin
X	${INSTALL_MAN} ${WRKSRC}/xpenguins.1 ${MANPREFIX}/man/man1
X
X.include <bsd.port.mk>
END-of-xpenguins/Makefile
echo x - xpenguins/distinfo
sed 's/^X//' >xpenguins/distinfo << 'END-of-xpenguins/distinfo'
XMD5 (xpenguins-1.2.tar.gz) = d87aabddc455d12d9ccffbe491fa3b5d
XMD5 (xpenguins-1.2-xpm_valuemask_fix.patch) = 8b60ac0bbec4711f32a46c269dd9106f
END-of-xpenguins/distinfo
echo c - xpenguins/files
mkdir -p xpenguins/files > /dev/null 2>&1
echo x - xpenguins/files/patch-aa
sed 's/^X//' >xpenguins/files/patch-aa << 'END-of-xpenguins/files/patch-aa'
X--- Makefile.orig	Thu May 18 06:00:20 2000
X+++ Makefile	Thu Jan 25 04:13:50 2001
X@@ -1,13 +1,13 @@
X # Makefile for XPENGUINS
X # Copyright (C) 1999, 2000  Robin Hogan
X 
X-CC = gcc
X+CC ?= gcc
X RPM_OPT_FLAGS =
X-CFLAGS = -Wall $(RPM_OPT_FLAGS) 
X+CFLAGS ?= -Wall $(RPM_OPT_FLAGS) 
X 
X XLIBS = -lX11 -lXpm -lXext
X-XLIBDIR = -L/usr/X11R6/lib -L/usr/local/lib 
X-XINCLUDEDIRS = -I/usr/X11R6/include -I/usr/local/include 
X+XLIBDIR = -L${X11BASE}/lib
X+XINCLUDEDIRS = -I${X11BASE}/include
X 
X OBJS = xpenguins.o toon.o
X PROGRAM = xpenguins
X@@ -17,7 +17,7 @@
X $(PROGRAM): $(OBJS)
X 	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(XLIBDIR) $(XLIBS) $(OBJS) -o $(PROGRAM)
X 
X-%.o: %.c
X+.c.o:
X 	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) $(XINCLUDEDIRS) -c $<
X 
X clean:
END-of-xpenguins/files/patch-aa
echo x - xpenguins/pkg-comment
sed 's/^X//' >xpenguins/pkg-comment << 'END-of-xpenguins/pkg-comment'
XCute little penguins that walk along the tops of your windows
END-of-xpenguins/pkg-comment
echo x - xpenguins/pkg-descr
sed 's/^X//' >xpenguins/pkg-descr << 'END-of-xpenguins/pkg-descr'
XXPenguins animates a friendly family of penguins in your root
Xwindow.  They drop in from the top of the screen, walk along
Xthe tops of your windows, up the side of your windows, up the
Xside of the screen, and sometimes even levitate with their
Xgenetically-modified go-go-gadget 'copter ability. Be careful
Xwhen you move windows as the little guys squash easily. If
Xyou send the program an interupt (such as by hitting Ctrl-C)
Xthey will burst.
X
XWWW: http://xpenguins.seul.org/
X
X- Michael Lyngbol lyngbol@candid.dk
END-of-xpenguins/pkg-descr
echo x - xpenguins/pkg-plist
sed 's/^X//' >xpenguins/pkg-plist << 'END-of-xpenguins/pkg-plist'
Xbin/xpenguins
END-of-xpenguins/pkg-plist
exit




-- 
KATO Tsuguru / tkato@prontomail.ne.jp
